name: Liliha Bakery
tagline: Coco puffs since 1950 — the green chiffon cake and butter rolls are the real insider order.
parking: Dedicated lot at the original Kuakini St location; Nimitz Hwy location has larger lot
talkStory: Founded in 1950 by Takeo and Sumie Takakuwa, a Japanese-American couple who built a neighborhood bakery that became a Honolulu institution. The Coco Puff — choux pastry filled with chocolate pudding and topped with Chantilly frosting — is arguably Hawaii's most iconic pastry. Locals just say 'I brought puffs' and everyone on Oahu knows exactly what that means. A box is the standard omiyage (gift) when visiting someone's home. Go before 7am weekdays for fresh batches and zero line — the butter rolls for a dollar are the quiet insider pick.
